behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model output quality from a real speech-to-text system (speaker diarization/label retention). A stubbed transcript would hide diarization mistakes. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on STT output preservation, not a required multi-agentic sequence assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript chunks retain stable speaker labels mapped to distinct attendees; fail if labels are dropped/collapsed or inconsistent enough to break attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic at a local function/object boundary with no required I/O. Rejected Integration: behavior text is about extraction logic itself, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixture transcripts containing explicit assignments and verify exact structured output fields (`assignee`, `task_text`) including multiple assignees and malformed lines.

behaviors.description: Live summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM decision quality (coverage/completeness of generated action items per assignee). Deterministic mocks would always pass this semantic requirement. Rejected Workflow: behavior text does not require validating multiple agentic stages in sequence, only final summary quality against known assignments.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with known assignee-task ground truth to the real summarization LLM and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output; run multiple trials to detect omission variance.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into `decisions` schema, not generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is routing/mapping logic for already-identified decisions; real infra or model output is not required to expose the failure. Rejected Integration: no explicit cross-module dependency in behavior wording beyond local transformation.
behaviors.test_description: Call decision-routing function with fixtures containing identified decision objects and assert they populate only `decisions` schema fields and are absent from narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key transcript decisions appear in live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the real LLM extracts and places decision content correctly; stubs cannot validate model judgment or placement quality. Rejected Workflow: wording targets summarizer output quality, not mandatory validation of multiple agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts with explicit key decisions to the real LLM summarizer and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section (and not only narrative text), with tolerance for wording variation.

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service splits into segments, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires multiple sequential agentic steps (per-segment transcription/summarization plus merge coherence). Single-call Agentic or Integration tests miss cross-step drift and merge-order failures. Includes explicit cross-module handoff/order state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, run full segmented pipeline with real model calls, and assert: segment boundaries respected, all segments processed in order, merged output is single structured summary with no missing/duplicated section content across boundaries.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes automatically for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end orchestration across multiple stages including real model-dependent steps; failures can emerge only across stage sequencing, retries, and handoffs. Lower levels cannot catch no-manual-intervention pipeline breaks.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end job with a real meeting file through production-like upload->STT->LLM summary->delivery path; assert terminal success, valid structured payload, and no manual recovery actions.
